title: aster canus subsp. punctatus
intro: Aster canus subsp. punctatus, also known as Spotted Chrysanthemum or Spotted Aster, is a flowering plant species in the daisy family (Asteraceae). Traditionally, it has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, derived from its bioactive compounds such as flavonoids and phenolic acids. The plant's health benefits include its potential to help soothe digestive issues, reduce fever, and alleviate pain, making it a valuable remedy in traditional medicine. In herbal preparations, Aster canus subsp. punctatus is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures that can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
